chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Appln No.2159 of 2025 ____________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(s) OF JUDGE(s) ____________________________________________________ For hearing of bail application 1. For Orders on M.A. No.11771/2025 2. For Orders on M.A. No.11772/2025 3. For hearing of Bail Application 25.08.2025 Mr. Muhammad Mushtaq, Advocate for the applicant a/w the applicant. ---------------------------- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Through this bail application the applicant / accused namely Mumtaz Hussain seeks protective bail in Crime No.02 of 2021 under Sections 4/5/23 FER Act, 1947 r/w 3/4 AML Act, 2010, and 109 PPC registered at Police Station FIA, State Bank Circle, Karachi. Learned counsel for the applicant contends that the applicant has been falsely implicated in the instant case, however, the applicant intends to surrender before the trial Court to obtain pre-arrest bail but apprehends his arrest at the doorstep of the Court at the hands of police in order to humiliate and disgrace him. Hence, he has prayed for protection. Without touching the merits and de-merits of the case, applicant/accused namely Mumtaz Hussain is granted protective bail for a period of seven working days from today enabling him to surrender before the trial Court subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. This protective bail shall remain in force for seven working days from today or on the date when the applicant will surrender before the trial Court, whichever is earlier.

Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. This protective bail shall remain in force for seven working days from today or on the date when the applicant will surrender before the trial Court, whichever is earlier. The Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE Asif
